titleStmt.title: Letter from T West to Lady Clonbrock, 31 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: T West

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from a prisoner of war, T West, Connaught Rangers, to Lady Clonbrock (1839-1928). West was incarcerated in Limburg, Germany at the time of this letter. He writes to thank her for the parcel she sent him, writing that he is in good health and hopes that she is keeping well.Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Kriegsgefangenenlager Limburg, 31st July 1916 Dear Lady Clonbrock Just a card to
let you know I received your last
parcel through the Irish Womens
Association quite safe & I may
say I am very thankful to you
for same. I am in very good health
as indeed are all the men here trust
âing you are well I remain yours
sincerely T.West This is Yr.Prisoner, not mine
Kriegsgefangenenlager
Limburg (Lahn).
This is my address: Name: T. West Conn. Rangers Name of the Regt. Regt. N o. 7258, II Batt. 4 Comp., N o. 622 Prisoner of War Camp Limburg (Lahn) Germany Right side Feldpostkarte.
Kriegsgefangenensendung Adresse: The Lady Clonbrock Ahascragh, Co.Galway Ireland.
